entry.title: Looking to Understand User Exits in SAP SD with Real-Time Case Studies
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working in the SAP SD module and looking to expand my knowledge in the area of User Exits, specifically within the Sales and Distribution (SD) domain.

I would like to mention that I am completely new to this functionality and eager to understand it from the ground up. I’ve come across the term "User Exit" quite often in discussions related to customization and enhancements, but I’m still unclear about:

  • What exactly are User Exits and how do they differ from BADIs or Enhancements?

  • Where and how are they implemented in SAP SD?

  • What are some real-time business use cases where User Exits have been effectively used to solve specific problems or meet business requirements?

If possible, I would greatly appreciate step-by-step explanations or references to detailed examples that can help a beginner like me understand this concept in depth. Any inputs, documents, or links would be very helpful!

Thanks in advance for your time and guidance.

Best regards,
Sobhagya Thapa

entry.title: Re: error when recording characterist on QA32: The entere...
entry.content:
Hi,

This warning occurs because the recorded value exceeds the upper limit defined in the inspection characteristic (MIC) master data or inspection plan. SAP gives you the chance to override it, but it warns every time.

Regards

entry.title: SAP Enable Now enablement: Important Updates for Consultants and Learners
entry.content:
SAP lately implemented several changes to streamline the SAP Enable Now-related enablement portfolio:

  1. Classroom Training:

    • Standard instructor-led training courses for SAP Enable Now are no longer available as of calendar week 22.
    • The lab infrastructure used for hands-on practice during these courses has been decommissioned.
    • Customers who still require training must now opt for customer-specific training sessions.
    • These customized training sessions will be conducted using the customer's own SAP Enable Now instance for hands-on exercises.

  2. E-learning Courses:

    • Existing e-learning courses (SEN100e, SEN200e, and SEN300e) will remain available on learning.sap.com.
    • However, these courses will no longer be maintained or updated.
    • For the interim solution 'SAP Enable Now - enablement content creation edition', new courses with reduced content will be provided.
    • The new course codes are SEN101e and SEN201e.
    • It's important to note that SEN300 (consulting) is not affected by these changes.

  3. Certification:

    • The consulting certification "C_SEN" has been officially retired.
    • Consultants who already hold this certification do not need to complete any "stay certified" assessments.
    • The 'certified consultant' status will be automatically extended for one year upon expiration.
    • After this extra year existing certification badges will remain visible on "Credly" and will maintain their validity until the official end-of-life of SAP Enable Now (which has not yet been scheduled). An "expired" flag will be added to show the inactive state.
    • No further action is required from certified consultants to maintain their status.

  4. Partner Enablement:

    • All partner-related enablement materials and content sources have been retired.
    • These resources are no longer available for access or use.

These changes ensure a focused approach to SAP Enable Now enablement services while accommodating the integration of WalkMe into SAP's product portfolio. Consultants with existing certifications benefit from automatic extensions and continued validity of their credentials. Customers seeking training can still receive tailored instruction using their own instances. The introduction of new e-learning courses for the interim solution "SAP Enable Now - enablement content creation edition" provides continuity in learning opportunities. It's important to note that SAP Enable Now product maintenance will continue without restrictions until further notice, and ongoing service engagements or planned implementations are not affected by these changes.

entry.title: How do I make the Line Item Text field reportable?
entry.content:
how do I make the Line Item Text field of sales order reportable in our custom dispatch report?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How do I make the Line Item Text field reportable?
entry.content:
Please refer to SAP KBA 0003282990

Resolution

Consider using API Sales Order (A2X) to extract the header and item texts for the sales orders:

  • /A_SalesOrderItem(SalesOrder='{SalesOrder}',SalesOrderItem='{SalesOrderItem}')/to_Text (Reads the text of a sales order item).
  • /A_SalesOrder('{SalesOrder}')/to_Text (Reads the header texts of a sales order).

 

There is currently an Influence Idea for this requirement: CDS View for Long Text. 

Within this influence request development have provided the following update:

"To offer the CDS view for long text, the text table need to be redesigned, all text data has to be migrated and the text data reading, writing, copying logic along the sales E2E process have to be updated. To make sure the replacement will work smoothly after the replacement, a proof of concept has been planned in the coming release. And the feature will be planned later based on the findings."

The next update on this will be Q2 2025.

entry.title: Have you ever heard a child say: ‘I want to become an SAP Consultant?’
entry.content:
Let’s be honest: No one dreams of becoming an SAP Consultant when they’re a child.
There’s no LEGO set for it. No cartoon character. No poster on the classroom wall - no movie.

Five years ago, SAP was a complete mystery to me – or rather, a book with cryptic chapters: S/4HANA, ABAP, PP-DS, APO, IBP or BTP.

Back then, as a humanities graduate with art background, I would’ve loved to hear someone say:
“Go for it – your perspective belongs here, too.”

Today, I know: SAP is the perfect playground for those who want to shape, not just execute.
It’s full of complex systems – but also full of potential for human-centric change.
For people who don’t just think in transactions, but in transformation.

Working primarily in the German-speaking SAP ecosystem, I’ve seen both the opportunities and the blind spots. The strong structures – and the missing stories.

I’m currently writing my first book for SAP Press, planning a doctoral research project, and hosting two podcasts for the SAP community:
🎙 SAP Sommeliers – for everyone who enjoys SAP with depth and delight, and
🍵 Alice in Serverland – my teaparty for strong women in SAP.

Myself now in my office and when I was a child.

But most importantly: I’m here to help others not just get started – but to find and live their role in the SAP ecosystem in a self-determined and strength-oriented way.
Especially those who still think: “SAP? That’s not for me.”

Because here’s the truth:
In Germany, most young people – and especially women – don’t actively aim for a career in SAP. They stumble into it.
It’s rarely a conscious choice, even though the opportunities are enormous.

With a growing skills shortage and serious talent gaps, we can’t rely on coincidence anymore.
We need to turn accidental careers into intentional ones.
And that means: Every SAP consultant should speak about what they do – outside the SAP bubble.
To make this profession more tangible, more human, more alive.

Because maybe, just maybe, the next generation will say:
“I want to become an SAP Consultant – because I’ve seen what’s possible.”

💡 Let’s make SAP more approachable for young minds and career changers – and build the kind of ecosystem where dreams don’t have to start in tech to end in impact.

👉 What opened the door for you?
👉 Who helped you see your place in this ecosystem?
👉 Who should we make more visible in the SAP Community?

Let’s connect and co-create.

Warmly,
Sarah

P.S.: This was my Hello-World-Post for the SAP Community Blog - I hope you enjoyed it 🙂

entry.title: Re: Matrix Grid Reports: Performance-Potential (or How vs...
entry.content:
Hello Sofia,

I hope your issue might have been resolved.

Please check on the setting in the matrix grid : Performance-Potential (or How vs. What).

Check under the settings : make sure the setting is enabled Exclude deleted forms when retrieving ratings.

On the other hand :

Managers have updated people profile on employees with data in the live system. The issue is the employees not visible in the 9 Box rating of Hows VS what.

First check : Make sure the option for Matrix grid report is enabled in provisioning.

As per experience if this switch is disabled all the matrix grid settings will be disabled in the instance.

Second check:  Enable the setting on the Matrix grid Hows vs What - Use average of all found ratings, instead of latest - this will add all the ratings and average out for the employee.

 

Regards,

Aashi

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Analytic Model in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
We recently moved from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere, 

Basic capabilities like object-level access control are missing. If multiple models exist in a space, all are exposed to users there’s no option to restrict access to specific models with in a Space. This is a critical gap in Data security. we will end up creating Hundreds of Space with the combination of models with Restriction in Real Business Scenarios

The data loading monitor is another major shortcoming. It's cluttered and lacks visibility, making it nearly impossible to track DataLoads.

I must say—One Should Reconsider Replacing the BW with Datasphere without retaining BW as the backbone.

entry.title: Full Stack ABAP Application In Build
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to make a full stack ABAP Information using SAP Build For using AI Features like :

1) ABAP Business Object Generation.

2) ABAP Joule 

But Facing Some issues, Please Provide some assistance.

entry.title: Re: Can I extend a certain app? With Clean Core? How?
entry.content:
Hi @BartvdKamp 

What you are trying to do is unfortunately not possible. The errors you are getting are the obvious ones which you already identified yourself: the CDSs in question are not released for extension in ABAP Cloud, thus the clear "not released and thus not permitted" error.

But that isn't the final problem. Say all the CDS were released, what you are trying to do still won't work. You are trying to add a filter of another CDS entity into the root entity; this can never work as selections on the root entity happen on the root entity alone and if you wanted to filter on any association or child entity, that filter has to be passed correspondingly via that entity, not on the root. Here an example (I had no malfunction reports in my demo system, so I'm using Purchase Requisition to showcase my meaning):

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaserequisition_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaserequisition/0001/PurchaseReqn?$filter=_PurchaseRequisitionItem/any(a:a/MaterialGroup eq 'A001')

With this, the service returns all PR that have at least one item which has material group A001. The any operator only works on "to many" relationships, not 1:1. When you have a 1:1 you can access it directly via path expression, for example:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaserequisition_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaserequisition/0001/PurchaseReqnItem?$filter=_PurchaseReqnDelivAddress/Region eq 'CA'

All PR Items that have Region CA.

So you see, technically you could filter with _MaintenanceOrder/ScheduledBasicEndDate and _MaintenanceOrder/ScheduledBasicStartDate (as long as the OData service in question is built to support all regular OData protocol filtering - that could potentially be a hinderance as well, especially when we talk about SEGW services). 

So coming to, what you can do? A few ideas come to mind:

  1. First of, confirm how the OData query would look like, as in my examples, and test in a REST tool or directly on the GW client. Make sure that what you are trying to do is possible at all. If the OData service does not support the filtering you need, sadly you are out of luck. Then I can only refer to customer influence ticket to request the functionality you are missing.
  2. If 1. was successful, check with a developer if they can create an adaptation project for the SAP standard Fiori app and add custom filters that way, ensuring that either the "any" or path expression is done correctly, as you tested in 1. (refer to Filtering - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK including how to do any). I'm not 100% sure this will work, but this is where I would try.
  3. If 1. and 2. didn't work, you could potentially looking at creating your own Fiori app, reusing the standard CDSs and adding again filtering via your Fiori app. I would only recommend this for pure read-access. If you need change/edit/delete etc. I do not recommend this as in a sense you are copying SAP standard functionality. If you need that, a potential workaround could be to navigate to the SAP standard app, once for example a malfunction report is clicked, and your app only takes care of the selection and list part. Not the nicest solution, but could be an option.

I hope this helps. This was a great question to showcase how every app and requirement can be a lot different 😀

entry.title: Re: EDIT HIRE RECORD BY IMPORT
entry.content:
Hi Hannah and everyone,
It is actually possible to edit a specific record via incremental import. In your import template csv file, you need to identify the record by providing the effective start date and sequence number, as well as the userId. This will result in an update of the record with this business key instead of creating a new record.
Best, Janina

However, I haven’t been able to identify which catalog contains the Web Dynpro app "Create Maintenance Order". Because of this, I’m not sure whether I'm maintaining the restrictions correctly.

  2. Is it possible to create a variant from a Web Dynpro app?

  3. Is it possible for the fields in this app to be auto-filled using the default values?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: SAC Story breaks when Datasphere model is updated
entry.content:
Hello,

This usually happens when a dimension/association/attribute has been unchecked and re-checked (added) at the analytic model level. After deployment, Datasphere will recreate a new internal ID for that dimension and it won't be recognized by the SAC story as the expected internal ID has changed for that object.

There might be other scenarios where that happens but that's the most common.

Regards,

Clément

entry.title: Through the Looking Glass: Manage Service Orders in Joule
entry.content:
Service Management and Joule have advanced their integration. After release of the first Joule case for service confirmations with OP2023 FPS3, the Joule release 04/25 now delivers first capabilities to manage service orders. We provide now a

  • Fast navigation to a service order
  • Search service orders
  • Release a single service order
  • Release a single service order item

Motivation and Scope

Joule is the central AI assistant provided by SAP. Joule enables users to execute tasks using fluent, easy-to-use language. The capabilities in Joule for managing service orders are a first basic step for a more simple, faster way to navigate, search and release single service orders and single service order items.

This is especially interesting for users like managers which are not very confident with the more detailed Manage Service Orders app in the Fiori Launchpad.

The capabilities for service orders are assigned to the role SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER (Service Manager). The ODATA API for service orders which is called by Joule handles all authorizations as of now.

Starting Screen

The starting screen provides provides a button which opens up the Search Manage Order screen in the background.

 

Fast Navigation

Joule allows users to open service orders with simple prompts like ‘Display Service Order [Service Order ID]’. This feature displays essential details such as service order ID, description, and Sold-to-Party, with an ‘Open’ button for direct access.

If the button is chosen, the service order object page opens up directly.

Search Service Orders

Searching for service orders is now streamlined. Users can employ prompts like ‘Display service orders [search criterion] [value]’ to find orders based on criteria such as Posting Timeframe, Created By, and Changed At. 

The currently supported search criteria are:

  • Posting Timeframe
  • Created By (System User)
  • Created At
  • Changed By (System User)
  • Changed At

The search results are organized by the newest posting dates, enhancing efficiency. The button opens up the service order in the background as described in the navigational case.

The number of all displayed and existing service orders, e.g. 6/18 (6 out of 18 found) is displayed.

The five delivered search criteria—Posting Timeframe, Created by, Created at, Changed by, and Changed at—can be combined. But in the prompt currently a search criterion can be used only once.

A complex prompt example like ‘Display service orders created by VOLLMERMI and created at 11.05.2022 and changed by ZHANGBOWE1 and changed at 12.09.2022’.

The search with the criterion Posting Timeframe allows to search for a specific year, month or day.

Examples are posted last year, this year’, in 2022, current month, today, yesterday, etc.

 

Release of a Service Order

A prompt like ‘Release service order [service order ID]’ asks for confirmation to release this specific service order. If the user confirms, it checks first if the service order is already released.

If it is not released, Joule attempts to release this specific service order. After the execution, a feedback and the Open button for navigation is given. Below is an example of a successful release.

The example below shows an error case. The first error message of the transaction is displayed as feedback.

The example below shows a release of a service order which is already released. As can be seen, Joule checks this first and reacts accordingly.

Release of a Service Order Item

A prompt like ‘Release item [item number] of service order [service order ID].’ asks for confirmation to release this specific service order item. If the user confirms, it checks first if the service order item is already released.

If it is not released, Joule tries to release this specific service order item. After the execution, a feedback and the Open button for navigation is given. Below an example of a successful release is given.

The error case and an already released service order item are handled similar as with a service order release.

Capability Limitations

While Joule offers significant improvements, there are limitations, Currently there are the following limitations.

Direct Navigation:

Prompts like ‘Open up service order 800023831 directly’ are currently not supported. The provided open button must always be pressed. It is not possible to open up the service order object page via prompting only.

 

Search service orders:

Same criterion cannot be used multiple times. ‘Example: Search service orders created by VOLLMERMI and created by ZHANGBOWE1.’

 

Release of service orders and service order items:

Releasing multiple service orders in one prompt is currently not supported.

Releasing multiple service order items in one prompt is currently not supported

Release of service orders and service order items together in one prompt t is currently not supported.

Direct release without confirming the change. The current concept of Joule is that the user should get a summary of the change and has to confirm it before execution.

Joule is currently not able to differentiate between header and item errors. This means the release of a service order item is currently an error case as well if there is an error in the transaction on the header or another item.

 

Outlook

Future updates aim to expand search criteria. We also aim to add additional fields to the search result list. We are thinking as well about additional info sections to drill down for further information by another click. Last but not least additional status changes ands a create service order use case is looked upon.

Conclusion:

Joule is paving the way for a more intuitive and efficient service management experience. As SAP continues to refine and expand Joule's capabilities, users can look forward to even more streamlined processes. Your feedback is invaluable, so feel free to share your thoughts and suggestions.

Thank you for exploring the new features with us. We hope this makes your service management tasks a little easier and more enjoyable.

Last but not least if you liked this blog and found it helpful, I am happy to receive your kudos.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – What is Key User Extensibility and Who are your Key Users

Excellent blog Jocelyn! well done! 

We are currently in the explore phase of an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (2408) implementation for a public sector organization in the UK. During discussions around reporting in G/L, A/P, A/R, and procurement we’ve been informed that several existing reporting requirements are not supported by standard SAP Fiori apps and have therefore been flagged as custom requirements. entry.title: Re: An error occurred while importing fixed asset posting...
entry.content:
Hello @yahaha 

To resolve this issue for ledger 0L, check and open the relevant periods in Asset Accounting for the specific depreciation area linked to ledger 0L:

Step-by-step solution (S/4HANA Public Cloud):

1.App: “Manage Fixed Asset Periods” (App ID: F1603)

◦ Use this app to open asset periods for the required company code and fiscal year.

2.Check the following:

◦ Ensure the company code is correct.

◦ Confirm that fiscal year 2025 is open for:

▪ Ledger 0L

The relevant depreciation area

◦ Make sure periods 01–12, or at least the current period, are marked as open.

3.App: “Define Depreciation Areas

◦ Verify which depreciation area is assigned to ledger 0L (e.g., area 01 or 10).

◦ Cross-check this with the information in the import error log.

4. Import again once all necessary periods are open in ledger 0L.

Note: Occasionally in S/4HANA Public Cloud, simulation runs may work in ledger 2L (e.g., for reporting), while 0L fails due to stricter local settings. Always use 0L as. your reference for period control.

Let me know if it works

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Save Time in SAP! Automate Repetitive Tasks with Script Recording - Test Data Creation
entry.content:
SAP consultants often face repetitive tasks, such as master data creation, test data creation, and transaction entries, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors when done manually.

Fortunately, SAP offers a powerful automation tool—SAP Script Recording—that allows users to record and replay repetitive processes, significantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy. Whenever creating test data or updating material records is required, script recording can streamline these tasks with minimal coding knowledge.

In this blog post, we’ll explore how to leverage SAP Script Recording for automatic test data creation, including a step-by-step guide to recording, editing, and executing scripts in SAP.

Step 1: Enable Script Recording in SAP (Transaction: RZ11)

If the Current value is FALSE, mention the new value as "TRUE" and save.

Step 2: Let's create a Reservation Test Data (Transaction: MB21)

Click on Customise Local Layout and navigate to "Script Recording and Playback"

Complete the reservation creation process, and then click on SAP GUI Scripting and stop the process

You will find this option bottom right of the screen

Step 3: Adding a Condition to the Script

Open the Script file in Notepad, and add the condition as below

For I = 1 to 3

Means, if you run this script 3 times reservation creation process will be carried out.

Step 4: Running the Script in SAP

Navigate to transaction MB21 (reservation creation screen)

Select the Script file path and click on Resume Playback, and watch as the script executes automatically - like magic! 🎩✨

Step 5: Adding Iteration Text in Reservation

Now, in the Goods Recipient field text must be different in each reservation creation process. Update the condition as below.

Repeat Step 4 to run the script

SAP Script will create reservations with different texts for each reservation in the Goods Recipient field as below

 

Regards,

Leelamohan

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Daniel Mateo
entry.content:
Hello SAP family! This blog is a part of the "Blog It Forward" challenge. Thanks to my friend and almost neighbor @Lluis, and his recent blog, for tagging me to participate in this great initiative for the first time.

My name is Daniel Mateo, and I’m an experienced SAP S/4HANA Project Manager based in a coastal city near to Barcelona, with a passion for transforming business financial operations  leveraging SAP’s capabilities to create real business value and guide organizations through their digital transformation journeys.

I began my career around 20 years ago at a Spanish IT consulting firm as an ABAP developer, but I quickly transitioned into functional roles within Finance and Logistics. Over time, I specialized in international SAP rollouts, with a strong focus on Finance and Controlling. For the past 12 years, I have been working as an SAP Project Manager, leading global implementations and transformations across various sectors such as pharma, manufacturing, food and beverages, mass media, and casinos, among others. Throughout the years, I’ve had the privilege of working with diverse teams in multiple countries—combining my passion for travel with my career in SAP.

In addition to managing complex implementations, I thrive on continuous learning and adapting to the evolving landscape of SAP technologies. My journey has been shaped by both professional achievements and personal experiences, such as relocating to Bulgaria during the pandemic and staying for three years. This allowed me to embrace new challenges while contributing to SAP projects remotely.

Fun Facts About Me

  • 🌍SAP and Backpacking: A Perfect Match
    My career in SAP has taken me across the globe, and I have made the most of it by exploring over 60 countries. Whether it is backpacking through Southeast Asia, road-tripping across Europe, or discovering hidden gems in South America, I love immersing myself in new cultures. Traveling has also given me a unique perspective in my work—understanding different business environments, adapting to diverse teams, and experiencing firsthand how SAP solutions impact companies worldwide.
  • 🇧🇬Bulgaria: An Unexpected Home
    When the pandemic hit, I made the spontaneous decision to relocate to Bulgaria. What started as a temporary move turned into a three-year stay. I got to experience the country’s rich history, vibrant tech scene, and stunning nature, from the Black Sea coast to the Rila Mountains. It was an incredible time for personal and professional growth, and it reinforced how flexible and borderless the SAP world can be.
  • 🏖Yoga and Beach: Recharging My Energy
    No matter the season—winter or summer—I always find time to enjoy the beach. There's something about the sound of the waves and the fresh sea breeze that resets my mind after long hours on SAP projects. The perfect combination? Practicing yoga by the sea. It’s my go-to way to recharge and stay centered, especially when juggling tight go-lives, complex configurations, and international time zones. 

 What do you most enjoy about the SAP community?

The SAP community is an incredible ecosystem of professionals who are passionate about technology and innovation. What I enjoy the most is the collaborative spirit—whether it's through groups, events, or online discussions, there's always someone ready to share knowledge and best practices.

It is inspiring to be part of a global network that is constantly evolving and sharing the trending topics to enhance the business transformation.

I regularly participate in SAP Inside Track events (mainly in Fortaleza and Barcelona), both as a speaker and an attendee. In addition, I maintain an active presence on LinkedIn, where I share insights and experiences related to SAP. I am also the co-author of a book in Spanish that details the recommended Best Practices in SAP S/4HANA rollouts, further contributing to knowledge sharing within the ecosystem.

What source do you use the most for learning SAP technologies?

I rely on a combination of official SAP resources and community-driven content. SAP Learning Hub and SAP Help Portal are excellent for structured learning. Additionally, SAP blogs, SAP Community discussions, and LinkedIn groups provide real-world insights and practical applications from experienced professionals. Keeping up with industry leaders and engaging in discussions helps me stay ahead of emerging trends in the SAP ecosystem.

What do you think are the key elements for a successful project?

A successful SAP project is built on three fundamental pillars:

  1. Clear Business Objectives: Aligning technology with business goals ensures that the solution delivers real value.
  2. Strong Governance & Stakeholder Engagement: Continuous collaboration between IT and business teams minimizes risks and ensures smooth decision-making.
  3. Effective Change Management :The success of a project is not just about technical implementation; it’s about how well users adopt the system. Training, communication, and proactive issue resolution are critical for long-term success.

What do you enjoy most in your work and why?

What I enjoy the most is driving business transformation. Seeing how SAP solutions optimize financial processes, improve efficiency, and empower companies to make better decisions is incredibly rewarding. Every project is unique, with its own challenges and opportunities, and that variety keeps the work exciting. Also, working on international projects allows me to engage with diverse teams and cultures, which is a great learning experience.

How do you survive when SAP keeps shifting our journey to new products?

SAP’s ecosystem is constantly evolving, and while it can feel overwhelming (especially with all the new acronyms, product renamings, and shifting roadmaps), the key to surviving is to position yourself as someone who doesn’t just follow the change, but helps lead it.

Here are a few survival strategies:

  1. Stay Curious and Keep Learning: New product? New cloud solution? New “RISE with” slogan? Dive into it. Take SAP Learning Hub courses, read blog posts, join SAP Community discussions. Even if you don’t implement it tomorrow, understanding what’s coming is power.

  2. Stick to Core Principles: While tools change, the fundamentals of business processes, integration, data governance, and project management stay solid. Focus on what delivers value to the business—technology is just the enabler.

  3. Build a Solid Network: Talk to other professionals, share experiences, learn from what’s worked (or not worked) in other projects. LinkedIn, SAP Community, and internal project retrospectives can be goldmines.

  4. Be Honest with Clients and Stakeholders: Don’t pretend to have all the answers. SAP sometimes doesn’t either! But if you can bring clarity to the ambiguity, people will trust you even more.

  5. Have a Sense of Humor: When a product name changes for the third time in a year, laugh. Then update your slide deck.

How do you see SAP technology in the next 5 years?

SAP technology is evolving rapidly, and I see three key trends shaping its future:

  1. Cloud-First & AI-Driven Solutions: SAP will continue pushing towards cloud-based ERP with AI-driven automation to enhance decision-making.

  2. Increased Integration & Interoperability: With the rise of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), we will see more seamless integration between SAP and non-SAP applications.

  3. Sustainability & Compliance: SAP is heavily investing in sustainability solutions, and I expect more tools helping companies track and manage their Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.

I hope you enjoy reading this blog as much as I enjoyed sharing my journey. Thanks for being part of the SAP community!

I would like to Blog it forward to @Marssel700 ,  a well-established SAP Champion with a strong finance background and creator of amazing courses, and to @AAncos , one of the top voices in the SAP Spanish-speaking community who truly deserves to be part of the SAP champions. I would suggest answering the question: With SAP's continuous push toward cloud-based solutions, how do you see the role of traditional SAP consultants evolving over the next 3–5 years? I'm sure they both have plenty of surprising insights and stories to share with us! 😉

entry.title: Re: Trust Configuration" in SAP BTP Trial Account – Unabl...
entry.content:
You can establish Trust with IAS tenant using BTP CLI:

Prerequisite: You have subscribed to IAS from one of your subaccounts.

Get BTP CLI (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-sapcp-getstarted..html)

Then, use the following sequence of commands.

btp login --sso
btp target
# select the subaccount by typing the number 
btp list security/available-idp
# if no IDP listed, see prerequsite
btp create security/trust --idp <first column from above>

entry.title: Re: Deprecation of Fields in Job Information (1H 2025)
entry.content:
Hi Anita and Jonas,
The event-reason field often isn't explicitly listed in "Manage Business Configuration", but it is always part of the Job Information data model because, as JC says, it is mandatory and holds essential information on why a particular change was made to someone's Job Information. But it only needs to be configured explicitly in "Manage Business Configuration" if you want to assign onChange rules to it or change its label.
The change-reason field is a remnant of Employee Central V1 and is no longer used by the system, i.e. it has no technical relevance. This is the field that we are deprecating.
Best regards,
Janina

entry.title: Re: Past picklist version doesn't show up in past jobinfo...
entry.content:
Hi Szilvia,
Across all Employee Central entities (such as Job Information), picklists aren't considered effective dated. This means that regardless of the Job Info record's effective start date, the system will always use the picklist version effective "today".
This is mentioned in the implementation guide here: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/implementing-picklists/picklist-versions?q=picklists&locale=en-US
Kind regards,
Janina

entry.title: Automatisez l’intégration des taux de marché dans SAP grâce à Market Rates Management
entry.content:
Introduction 

Dans un contexte économique en constante évolution, disposer de données financières fiables et actualisées est un véritable enjeu pour les entreprises. C’est précisément là qu’intervient le service SAP Market Rates Management, une solution proposée par SAP pour automatiser l’intégration des taux de marché dans les systèmes SAP.

Durant mes projets d’implémentation SAP en tant que lead technique, j’ai souvent été confronté à l’intégration de taux de change provenant de sources externes spécifiques développées sur mesure, sans recourir au service standard SAP Market Rates. Aujourd’hui, avec l’évolution de SAP BTP et l’arrivée de services comme celui-ci, il devient plus simple et plus sécurisé d’automatiser ces flux via une approche standardisée.

Ce blog marque le début d’une série d’articles autour des services SAP disponibles sur la SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). On commence avec un focus sur l’activation et l’intégration du service Market Rates avec SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

 

Nous verrons d’abord les différentes options proposées par SAP pour la gestion des données de marché, avant de plonger dans les étapes techniques d’activation via SAP BTP, puis de configuration dans un environnement SAP S/4HANA.

Le service SAP Market Rates Management permet d’automatiser l’acquisition, la gestion et l’intégration des données de marché dans vos environnements SAP. Il centralise les taux de change, indices financiers et autres données nécessaires à la gestion de trésorerie, à la comptabilité et aux rapports financiers.

SAP propose deux approches principales pour alimenter ces données :

  1. Refinitiv (anciennement Thomson Reuters)
    Une solution clé en main fournie par SAP, qui s’appuie sur une source de données financière globale. Elle garantit une actualisation automatique et fiable des taux directement depuis le marché.
  2. Bring Your Own Rates (BYOR)
    Une option qui permet aux clients d’importer leurs propres données depuis des sources internes ou partenaires, via des intégrations personnalisées.

 

Dans ce blog, nous nous concentrerons exclusivement sur l’option Refinitiv, qui permet une intégration rapide et standardisée. L’approche BYOR fera l’objet d’un prochain article.

 

 

Activation du service dans SAP BTP

L’activation du service SAP Market Rates Management se fait via le cockpit SAP BTP. Voici les étapes à suivre pour le mettre en place dans votre sous-compte Cloud Foundry

  1. Activation des services nécessaires

Activez les deux plans de service suivants (à adapter selon votre environnement) :

  • Refinitiv  (market-rates-th): service principal pour la gestion des données de marché
  • SAP Market Rates Management  (market-rates-ui 😞 interface utilisateur (tuiles Fiori)
  1. Création de l’instance et de la clé de service
  • Créez une instance pour chaque plan via le cockpit
  • Générez une clé de service pour obtenir les informations d’authentification OAuth nécessaires (Client ID, Secret, URL, scopes...).

 

Les paramètres Client ID, Client Secret ainsi que l’URL (complétée par le suffixe /oauth/token) seront utilisés pour la génération du token d’authentification OAuth2.

 Génération du token

  • Via Postman :
    Configurez une requête de type POST vers l’URL d’authentification avec les identifiants fournis dans la clé de service. Cela vous permettra de récupérer un token temporaire pour tester les appels API manuellement.
  • Depuis SAP S/4HANA :
    La connexion se configure dans la transaction SM59, via une destination de type HTTP (Type G) que nous voyons après en détail.
  1. Affectation des rôles

Attribuez les rôles requis à l’utilisateur (via la Role Collection Market-Rates-User) afin d’accéder à l’interface Fiori du service.

 

Une fois le service actif, l’application SaaS propose trois tuiles principales dans votre Launchpad Fiori :

  • « Configure Default Values »
    Paramètres généraux tels que la durée de rétention des données ou les options d’archivage.
  • « Configure Favorites »
    Sélection des paires de devises ou indices favoris pour chaque utilisateur. Ces favoris seront ensuite affichés dans la vue principale.
  • Display Market Rates
    Visualisation en temps réel des taux de marché récupérés via le service Refinitiv.

 

 

 Lors de la navigation dans l’application, les données sont téléchargées dynamiquement à partir de SAP BTP vers l’interface, selon les instruments sélectionnés.

 

 

Passons maintenant à l’intégration côté système SAP S/4HANA, ici en version On-Premise. Le processus repose sur la configuration d’une acquisition de données via RFC HTTP, combinée à un mapping des instruments financiers.

🔹Remarque : cette intégration s’appuie sur la note SAP suivante :
👉SAP Note 2431370 – Market Data Integration with BTP

 

Étapes de configuration dans SAP GUI

🧭 Customizing : accès

Accédez au chemin suivant dans SPRO :

SAP Financial Supply Chain Management

> Treasury and Risk Management

> Market Data Management

> Data Acquisition

  1. Définir une acquisition de données
  • Créez un identifiant (Data Provider) qui représentera la connexion à Refinitiv.
  • Exemple : « Refinitiv »
  1. Créer une destination RFC (SM59)
  • Type de connexion : G (HTTP vers un serveur externe)
  • URL hôte : celle indiquée dans la clé de service
  • Port : 443
  • Chemin : /oauth/token
  • Authentification :
    • Nom d’utilisateur : Client ID (depuis la clé)
    • Mot de passe : Secret (depuis la clé)
  • SSL : activé avec certificat client standard

⚠️Lors du test de connexion, un code HTTP 400 peut apparaître. Il est attendu et ne bloque pas la suite.

  1. Associer la destination RFC à l’acquisition
  • Programme partenaire : TB_DATAFEED_HCP_ACCESS
  • Mode : Synchrone
  • Destination RFC : celle créée en étape 2
  • URI : downloadURL (dans la clé de service)
  • Scope OAuth : depuis la clé

 

  1. Définir les sources de données

Exemples de sources :

  • ST – données globales Refinitiv fournies par SAP
  1. Configurer les devises et instruments

Pour chaque paire de devises à intégrer :

  • Type d’instruction : 01 – Devises
  • Exemple :
    • EUR~USD:01
    • CHF~JPY:01
  • Propriété du taux : BID, MID, ASK, CLOSE...
  • Taux de variation maximal autorisé (%), sauvegarde permanente, etc.

Une fois l’ensemble des étapes finalisées, votre système SAP est prêt à consommer automatiquement les taux de marché fournis par SAP via Refinitiv. Ces données sont désormais accessibles depuis la transaction TB48 ou exploitées directement dans vos processus métiers tels que le paiement, la conversion de devises ou la valorisation d’actifs.

Dans le cas d’un environnement SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP met également à disposition une tuile Fiori dédiée pour la visualisation et le suivi des taux intégrés

 

 

Le service SAP Market Rates Management simplifie considérablement l’intégration des données financières dans SAP. Grâce à une approche standardisée et sécurisée, il vous permet de vous affranchir des imports manuels ou des intégrations maison souvent lourdes à maintenir.

C’est un excellent point d’entrée pour exploiter les services cloud de SAP BTP dans des contextes hybrides ou full cloud.

entry.title: SAP EWM – Addition and removal of deliveries and deletion of TU
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management), a Transportation Unit (TU) represents a physical vehicle or transport container (like a truck, trailer, or railway wagon) that is used for inbound or outbound deliveries. It acts as a logistical object that helps in tracking and managing the movement of goods into and out of the warehouse.

In this blog, I would like to describe the process programmatically:

  • Restriction of addition and removal of deliveries against Transportation Unit (TU)
  • Addition of deliveries in TU
  • Removal of deliveries in TU
  • Deletion of TU

Restriction of addition and removal of deliveries against TU

  • In a Transportation Unit (/SCWM/TU), deliveries can be added or removed till loading is completed.
  • Once the loading completed status is checked in a TU, deliveries cannot be added or removed.

Addition of deliveries in TU

  • In this process, the standard class /SCWM/CL_SR_BOM and /SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU is utilized.
  • Internal TU Number and SR Number is passed in passed in reference to class /SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU in method GET_BO_TU_BY_KEY.
  • ADD_TU_DLV method is triggered in class /SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU for delivery addition.
  • LT_BO_DLV_ADD is passed in exporting parameter which contains the fields,

            tu_num – Internal TU Number

            tu_sr_act_num – SR Activity Number

            seq_num – Sequential number (Item number)

            docid – Reference document ID (Fetched from /SCDL/DB_REFDOC table)

            doccat – ‘PDO’ (Refers to Outbound Delivery – Hardcoded)

            docno – Delivery document number

  • LT_BO_TU_DLV_SUCCESS and LT_BO_TU_DLV_NO_SORT_SUCCESS tables are passed in importing parameter which returns the success values.
  • SAVE method is triggered in class /SCWM/CL_SR_BOM to save the added deliveries against the TU.
  • Finally, BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT is performed to reflect the data in database.

Removal of deliveries in TU

  • The same steps described above applies for removal of deliveries except the method called.
  • DEL_TU_DLV method is triggered in class /SCWM/CL_SR_BO_TU for delivery removal.
DATA: gr_sr_bom                      TYPE REF TO /scwm/cl_sr_bom,
      gr_bo_tu                       TYPE REF TO /scwm/cl_sr_bo_tu,
      ls_tu_act                      TYPE /scwm/s_tu_sr_act_num,
      lt_bo_dlv_add                  TYPE /scwm/tt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort,
      lt_bo_dlv_dlt                  TYPE /scwm/tt_bo_tu_dlv,
      lt_bo_tu_dlv_success           TYPE /scwm/tt_bo_tu_dlv,
      lt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success   TYPE /scwm/tt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort,
      lt_bo_tu_dlv_success_d         TYPE /scwm/tt_bo_tu_dlv,
      lt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success_d TYPE /scwm/tt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort.

"get TU internal number
SELECT SINGLE tu_num
FROM /scwm/tunit
INTO (lv_int_no)
WHERE tu_num_ext = 'XXX'. "(Shipment number)

"get SR act number
SELECT SINGLE tu_sr_act_num, lgnum
FROM /scwm/tu_dlv
INTO (ls_sr_act_num)
WHERE tu_num = _int_no.

"get document ID of delivery
SELECT refdocno,docid
FROM /scdl/db_refdoc
INTO TABLE (lv_refdoc)
WHERE refdocno = 'YYY'. "(Delivery document number)

TRY.
    gr_sr_bom = /scwm/cl_sr_bom=>get_instance( ).

    ls_tu_act-tu_num = lv_int_no.
    ls_tu_act-tu_sr_act_num = ls_sr_act_num-tu_sr_act_num.

    gr_bo_tu = gr_sr_bom->get_bo_tu_by_key( is_tu_sr_act_num = ls_tu_act ).

    ""Delivery addition
    lt_bo_dlv_add = VALUE #( ( tu_num        = |{ lv_int_no ALPHA = IN }|
                               tu_sr_act_num = |{ ls_sr_act_num-tu_sr_act_num ALPHA = IN }|
                               seq_num       = '1'
                               docid         = lv_refdoc
                               doccat        = 'PDO'
                               docno         = 'ZZZ' ) ). "Delivery docno to be added

    TRY.
        gr_bo_tu->add_tu_dlv(
        EXPORTING
          iv_action_only               = abap_true
          it_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort         = lt_bo_dlv_add
        IMPORTING
          et_bo_tu_dlv_success         = lt_bo_tu_dlv_success
          et_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success = lt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success ).

      CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error INTO DATA(lx_sr_error).
        DATA(lv_text) = lx_sr_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    ""Delivery removal
    lt_bo_dlv_dlt =  VALUE #( ( tu_num        = |{ lv_int_no ALPHA = IN }|
                               tu_sr_act_num = |{ ls_sr_act_num-tu_sr_act_num ALPHA = IN }|
                               seq_num       = '1'
                               docid         = lv_refdoc
                               doccat        = 'PDO'
                               docno         = 'ZZZ' ) ). "Delivery docno to be removed

    TRY.
        gr_bo_tu->del_tu_dlv(
          EXPORTING
            it_bo_tu_dlv                 = lt_bo_dlv_dlt
          IMPORTING
            et_bo_tu_dlv_success         = lt_bo_tu_dlv_success_d
            et_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success = lt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success_d ).
      CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error INTO DATA(ls_error).
        DATA(lv_error) = ls_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.


    TRY.
        gr_sr_bom->save( ).
        COMMIT WORK AND WAIT .
      CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error INTO lx_sr_error.
        lv_text = lx_sr_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.​

Deletion of TU

  Restrictions:

  • Transportation Unit can be deleted till loading is completed.
  • Once the loading completed status is checked in a TU, TU cannot be deleted.
  • If the arrival at checkpoint and loading started status has been checked, it should be reversed before the TU deletion.

Steps for TU Deletion

  • If the arrival at checkpoint and loading started status is checked, it should be reversed.
  • To reverse the status, status is read is for the TU using method READ_STATUS in class /SCWM/CL_SR_DB_TU.
  • TU Number and TU SR Activity number is passed in exporting parameter, and status is returned from importing parameter.
  • To reverse arrival at checkpoint status, /SCWM/IF_SR_BO~CHECK_IN_OUT method is called and SAVE method is triggered.
  • To reverse loading status, SET_TU_LOAD_UNLOAD method is called and SAVE method is triggered.
  • Next, deliveries should be removed for the TU.
  • To get the associated deliveries and reference document ID for a TU, data is selected from /SCWM/TU_DLV and /SCDL/DB_REFDOC table.
  • For deliveries removal, DEL_TU_DLV method is triggered and SAVE method is triggered.
  • Finally, to delete the TU DELETE method is triggered and SAVE method is triggered.
 ""To read the status of TU
    DATA(lr_/scwm/cl_log)      = NEW /scwm/cl_log( iv_nolog = abap_true ).

    DATA : lt_status TYPE /scwm/tt_tu_status.

    /scwm/cl_sr_db_tu=>read_status( EXPORTING
            it_tu_sr_act  =  VALUE #( ( tu_num        = 'XXX'     "Internal TU Number
                                        tu_sr_act_num = 'YYY'  ) )"SR Activity Number
            io_log        = lr_/scwm/cl_log
          IMPORTING
            et_tu_status  = lt_status ).

    LOOP AT lt_status INTO DATA(ls_status) WHERE status_value = abap_true.
      CASE ls_status-status_type.
        WHEN 'ISR19'.
          "reverse the status of arrival at checkpoint
          IF ls_status-status_value = abap_true.
            TRY.
                gr_bo_tu->/scwm/if_sr_bo~check_in_out(
                  EXPORTING
                    iv_chkpt  = 'CHKN'               " Checkpoint in Yard
                    iv_act    = 'SR28'               " Business Transaction
                ).
              CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error.
            ENDTRY.
            gr_sr_bom->save( ).
          ENDIF.

        WHEN 'ISR07'.
          "reverse the status of loading started
          IF ls_status-status_value = abap_true.
            TRY.
                gr_bo_tu->set_tu_load_unload(
                  EXPORTING
                    iv_check_only = abap_false       " Data Element for Domain BOOLE: TRUE (='X') and FALSE (=' ')
                    iv_activity   = 'SR14'           " Business Transaction
                ).
              CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error.
            ENDTRY.
            gr_sr_bom->save( ).
          ENDIF.
      ENDCASE.
    ENDLOOP.

    "To get associated deliveries
    SELECT tu_num, tu_sr_act_num,seq_num, ltrim( docno, '0' ) AS docno
    FROM /scwm/tu_dlv
    WHERE tu_num = _int_no AND
          tu_sr_act_num = _sr_act_num-tu_sr_act_num
    INTO TABLE (lt_tu_deliveries).

    DATA(lt_tu_deliveries_temp) = lt_tu_deliveries.
    DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_tu_deliveries_temp COMPARING tu_num tu_sr_act_num docno.

    IF lt_tu_deliveries_temp IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT refdocno,docid
      FROM /scdl/db_refdoc
      INTO TABLE (lt_tu_refdoc)
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN _tu_deliveries_temp
      WHERE refdocno = _tu_deliveries_temp-docno.
    ENDIF.

    lt_bo_dlv_dlt = VALUE #( FOR ls_tu_refdoc IN lt_tu_refdoc "INDEX INTO lv_index
                                     ( tu_num        = |{ lv_int_no ALPHA = IN }|
                                       tu_sr_act_num = |{ ls_sr_act_num-tu_sr_act_num ALPHA = IN }|
                                       seq_num       = VALUE #( lt_tu_deliveries[ docno = ls_tu_refdoc-refdocno ]-seq_num OPTIONAL ) "lv_index
                                       docid         = ls_tu_refdoc-docid
                                       doccat        = 'PDO'
                                       docno         = |{ ls_tu_refdoc-refdocno ALPHA = IN }| ) ).
    "To remove deliveries
    TRY.
        gr_bo_tu->del_tu_dlv(
          EXPORTING
            it_bo_tu_dlv                 = lt_bo_dlv_dlt
          IMPORTING
            et_bo_tu_dlv_success         = lt_bo_tu_dlv_success_d
            et_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success = lt_bo_tu_dlv_no_sort_success_d ).
      CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error.
        gr_sr_bom->save( ).
    ENDTRY.

    "To delete TU
    TRY.
        gr_bo_tu->delete(
          EXPORTING
            iv_check              = abap_false
            iv_called_by_ldap_req = abap_false       " Data element for domain BOOLE: TRUE (='X') and FALSE (=' ')
        ).
      CATCH /scwm/cx_sr_error.
    ENDTRY.
    gr_sr_bom->save( ).
    COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.​

Conclusion

Effectively adding or removing deliveries from a Transportation Unit in SAP EWM helps ensure smooth and flexible warehouse operations. It allows for better planning, optimized vehicle usage, and supports real-time adjustments to meet changing logistics needs.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
SAP is at the forefront of AI innovations, with Joule - SAP's AI assistant - revolutionizing the management of HR tasks within SuccessFactors. Joule provides intelligent support for HR processes, facilitating quick answers, task automation, and streamlined decision-making.

This blog presents a practical guide on deploying Joule in SAP SuccessFactors. The instructions are clear and accessible for both functional and technical teams.

Before You Begin Ensure that you have:

  • An active SAP SuccessFactors License that includes access to SAP Joule.
  • A global account in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
  • Successfully integrated SuccessFactors with SAP Cloud Identity Services for secure user logins.

Step 1: Establish Trust Between SAP BTP and Identity Authentication This setup allows Joule to authenticate users through Identity Authentication.

  • Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
  • Go to Security → Trust Configuration.
  • Establish Trust by adding your IAS tenant as a trusted identity provider.

Step 2: Configure User Attributes in IAS Joule requires basic user details like first name, last name, and email for personalization.

  • Navigate to the IAS Administration Console.
  • Select User Management → User Attributes.
  • Define and map attributes such as email, firstName, and lastName.

Step 3: Launch Joule Booster in SAP BTP Use SAP's automated booster to simplify Joule configuration.

  • Access the SAP BTP Cockpit with global admin privileges.
  • Click on Boosters.
  • Locate and start the "Setting up Joule" booster.
  • Follow the steps to choose the subaccount and review entitlements.

Step 4: Add Trusted Domains in IAS for SuccessFactors Enable secure communication between SuccessFactors and Joule.

  • In the IAS Admin Console, go to Applications & Resources → Applications.
  • Select the SAP SuccessFactors application.
  • In the Trust tab, add the domain utilized by your SuccessFactors system.

Step 5: Assign Joule Access via Permission Roles in SuccessFactors Grant relevant permissions to users for Joule access.

  • Log in to SuccessFactors and navigate to Admin Center.
  • Manage Permission Roles.
  • Choose roles requiring Joule access.
  • Activate Joule Access checkbox under User Permissions.

Step 6: Test and Confirm Integration Ensure Joule functions correctly post-setup.

Test Procedures:

  • Log in to SAP SuccessFactors with appropriate permissions.
  • Locate the Joule icon or chat assistant.
  • Engage with Joule by inputting queries to validate responses.

In Conclusion By adhering to these steps, you can seamlessly integrate Joule into SAP SuccessFactors, enhancing HR workflows with AI capabilities. This integration amplifies user engagement and efficiency through intelligent insights and automation, whether for full deployment or a pilot group.

Review the official SAP docs here.

Joule

entry.title: Integrating 3PL REST API with SAP Public Cloud (1ZQ Scope Item) Without Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are currently working on a customer implementation in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and facing an integration challenge with a 3rd Party Logistics (3PL) provider.

The customer has activated scope item 1ZQ - Third-Party Logistics (3PL) Integration. However, they do not have access to SAP Integration Suite (BTP) or any middleware in between.

The 3PL partner only provides a REST API, whereas the standard SAP 1ZQ process seems to expect SOAP-based integration (via WSDL, etc.). Moreover, the 3PL API structure does not match the expected data structures in SAP's standard outbound/inbound interfaces — meaning we need field-level and structure-level mapping.

Since we're working strictly within the boundaries of the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, we cannot implement classical ABAP enhancements or use custom middleware-based transformations.

Key Questions:
How can I map and transform the REST API payloads to match SAP’s expected SOAP message structure within the Public Cloud?

Is there any workaround or best practice to enable a transformation layer (without Integration Suite)?

Can we implement REST-to-SOAP mediation via custom communication arrangements or service consumption models?

Has anyone dealt with 3PL integrations in Public Cloud where the partner system only supported REST, and not SOAP?

Any guidance, experience, or documentation references would be highly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP EWM - Multi Step Internal VAS Order
entry.content:
VAS (Value Added Services) used to add value into products when they are received or while dispatching products to End Customer. In SAP EWM mostly we observe the VAS is followed in Inbound processes before Putaway. 

But if customer doesn't want to perform VAS while receiving the products or during the dispatch process. Rather VAS to be performed internally in a Warehouse and those products will be stored in Final Bins, and from where it will be picked to fulfil customer order without any delay. This process is extensively observed in 3PL Fulfilment Centers of Automotive, Garment, Food and Processing Industries. 

This blog is intended to showcase the VAS process handled with Internal VAS order in SAP EWM. 

Business Scenario: Product will be received in a warehouse and store directly into Final Storage types. VAS should be performed internally in a warehouse and after that products will be stored in picking area so that they are ready for dispatch as per order. 

Configuration:

1) Define VAS Order type for Internal Processes

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Value-Added Services (VAS) > Define Order Types for VAS for Internal Warehouse Processes

2) Define Document type and Item Type for Internal Stock Transfer 

SCM EWM > EWM >  Internal Warehouse Processes > Delivery Processing > Stock Transfers > Define Document Types for the Stock Transfer Process

SCM EWM > EWM > Internal Warehouse Processes > Delivery Processing > Stock Transfers > Define Item Types for the Stock Transfer Process

3) Define Warehouse Process Type

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Task > Define Warehouse Process Type

4) Define Work Center

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center > Define Work Center

5) Define Process-Oriented Storage Control

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross Process Settings > Warehouse Task > Define Process-Oriented Storage Control 

i) Storage Process- Definition 

ii) Assign Storage Process Steps

iii) Process- Oriented Storage Control

6) Define Putaway Strategies

SCM EWM > EWM > Goods Receipt Process > Strategies > Storage Type Search > Specify Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway

- This is maintained to perform IB03 (Putaway) step to store products in storage type after VAS, from where the products will be picked. In STSS assign the storage types where the stock will be moved after VAS.

7) Warehouse Order Creation Rule 

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Creation Rules for Warehouse Orders

-Define WOCR and assign storage process to it. 8 ) Determine WOCR

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross Process Settings > Warehouse Order > Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

Master Data:

i) Create Storage Bins and Assign to Work Center (/N/SCWM/TWORKST)

ii) Maintain Packaging Specifications (/N/SCWM/PACKSPEC)

- Create Packspec and maintain condition records.

- Maintain Levels and Packaging materials which will be used at each level. Also maintain external step at each level. 

iii) Maintain Product master (/N/SCWM/MAT1)

- Maintain check box to consume Auxiliary material while repacking into new Plastic Tray bin from Box. 

iv) Assign Storage Bins for VAS Consumption Posting (/N/SCWM/73000001)

Testing: 

1) Create VAS Order for Internal  Warehouse operations (/N/SCWM/VAS_INT)

i) Click on Create and then Select and Create Stock.  

ii) Enter mandatory parameters such as VAS order type, Pack. Spec number, and Product and click on execute.

iii) System will propose the available stock for the product. Enter the quantity or select complete checkbox and click on continue. 

iv) VAS order Created. Click on Save.

v) To process VAS order/ To move stock towards workcenter and Internal stock transfer request is created automatically on Saving VAS order. Click on Display Warehouse Req. 

- Click on Follow-on-Functions > Warehouse task. A warehouse task will be created to move material from Storage type to 1st VAS Workcenter. This WT creation can be automated using PPF. 

vi) Confirm Warehouse task and order. 

Vii) Execute VAS 1st Step

a) Go to first VAS Workcenter (/N/SCWM/VASEXEC) and execute.

b) Perform VAS Step. Once step is performed VAS order status for Workcenter 1 will be completed and we can move pallet to next workcenter. Select pallet and click on Complete process step for HU.

A Warehouse task is created automatically for moving material to next workstation. User should confirm this task manually. 

Viii) Execute VAS 2nd step

a) Go to second VAS Workcenter (/N/SCWM/VASEXEC) and execute.

b) Perform VAS Step.

- Click on Start, Enter Packaging material to repack product into Plastic tray bin. Repack product and HU label will be generated in background automatically and we can paste HU Label on tray bin. Click on End Process and save. 

- Once step is performed VAS order status for Workcenter 2 will be completed and we can move pallet to Final storage. Select pallet and click on Complete process step for HU.

- A Material document with movement type 291 is posted for the auxiliary material after completing the VAS step. 

- A warehouse task is created automatically to move products to Final storage from where the pickers will pick products for dispatch orders.

entry.title: Re: Cannot deploy iFlow due to warning.
entry.content:
Hi Jainee Even I faced the same issue, I checked the entire flow once again, then i realized my mistake that in Content Modifier Step Unde

Message Header Tab, in Data Type field I entered java.lan.String, where I missed g (correct data type: java.lang.String), finally when

- on company / team / project level
